title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More

title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More
body: I enjoyed hearing your impressions of UNM and seeing some of my favorite spots.  I was an out of state student with an Amigo Scholarship, class of ‘92. I loved my time as a Lobo.  My kid is there now, also with an Amigo Scholarship.   And I agree, UNM is a great diverse campus where everyone can find their passion.

title: University of New Mexico (UNM) | Youniversity 14: Campus Tour and More

Are there any other scholarships similar to the amigos for other universities in the US as I would love to keep my options for next year open. Cheers

body:

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: The restaurant business is BRUTAL.. you work your ass off for not much money… I just hope his love of cooking makes up for it..
author: @Madmaxx0829

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: I ate here last year. It was incredibly fancy, but just wasn't for me. It was right around $300 per person and they brought you multiple plates over a couple hour span. The food wasn't really anything bizarre. It was mostly all minimalist plates made with normal everyday ingredients, just cooked and stylized incredibly well. When I say minimalist plates I mean like a single piece of asparagus on a plate with a tiny half teaspoon of sauce on the side. I showed up hungry and left several hours later even hungrier. It was an interesting experience, but was it worth the price? Fuuuuuuuuuuuuck no. This is a place for rich people to take their rich friends to impress each other and nothing more.
author: @bigdeuce66

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: Pre Covid I worked L’atelier Joel robuchon in nyc as a barback. I even developed a few cocktails on the menu. During the lockdown I landed a job in tech but I still dream of working in a kitchen again like that. Funnest job I’ve ever had. Keep it going!
author: @jonfouquier

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: I went to culinary school with Chef Marissa. If you see her again tell I said hello. From Logan Pettinato

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: What an interesting life to lead— people so often forget what all goes into what is one the plate in from of them… myself included! Thx for sharing! And thx for your hard work :)
author: @Me12464626

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: I was a culinary student back in 1998. I tried it for a year, but then switched over to the front of the house. I was more successful at FOH than BOH; it was a rough life because I attended college while working. I graduated in 2009 at the age of 33. Better late than never. I am grateful for being able to put myself through school through working in restaurants and catering.
author: @joshuacooper1899

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: I miss the kitchen. I was a chef de cuisine for many years. I stepped back from my position late spring to stay home with babies. You’re doing great!! ❤

title: COLLEGE MOVE IN DAY VLOG | NMSU | PIÑON HALL | Anayancy Campos
body: I never been to NMSU (Las Cruces), but I've been to UNM (Albuquerque) and NM Tech (Socorro).  We live in Shiprock, NM.  What made you choose NMSU over other schools?  Are you from New Mexico or Texas?  What is your major?  I think you have now been at NMSU for at least 2 years.  How is it?  Do you like it?  Do you have an internship?  Would you recommend NMSU?  My daughter will graduate from San Juan College High School in Farmington, New Mexico, in May 2023 with a high school diploma and an Associates Degree in Engineering;  when she does, she might go there.  The way I was told by her counselor is that she already has a scholarship to go to UNM.  But I really don't know where she will go to a university.  I am thinking in California or New Mexico.
author: @harrisonlapahie3483
